message.content: Scrollback in CC eats the page after a long session. Diagnose.

content.thinking: This is the ghost-chrome leak issue. CC re-renders its TUI on every redraw and tmux dutifully appends those repaints to scrollback. Need to read the perf hot paths and confirm before suggesting a fix.

content.text: Likely the ghost-chrome leak — CC's TUI repaints itself and tmux appends each repaint to scrollback. After 30 min of conversation you've got megabytes of duplicate prompt-frames sitting in `capture-pane -p`. Let me check the scrollback viewer for hot paths.

content.content:
43: Five extras-row keys for taming CC's primary-buffer ghost-chrome leak
78: capture-pane → archive → clear
93: 📦 + ghost-chrome filter

content.text:
OK — there's already a hand-rolled filter in tmux-web that strips repeated prompt-frames. Pattern: `📦` archives current state to an HTML overlay then clears tmux history; `✂` does the same but runs the ghost-chrome filter first.

For your case I'd reach for `✂` — keeps the legible parts of the conversation in the archive, drops the visual noise. Bound to the extras row in `tmux-web/public/index.html` (search for `scrollback-viewer`).

message.content: Does the new wpin7 view have the same problem?

content.text:
No — wpin7 sources from panel's cell-diff stream, not tmux scrollback. Each cell has an mtime; rows that haven't changed simply aren't repainted in DOM. The leak class doesn't apply.

The leak only bites when you're reading the raw `capture-pane` output (📜 snapshot, 📦 archive). The default cell-grid view is also immune for the same reason.
